# Calma

> Calma is an open-source verifier for AI-generated computational results. It re-runs the code in
> a network-off sandbox, recomputes the headline number from the raw output files (never the
> reported number), and diffs it against the claim under a calibrated tolerance. The verdict comes
> from a single deterministic function, not a language model — so there is no score left to game.
> Calma proves the recompute; it does NOT prove the upstream input data is authentic or that the
> result is semantically correct.

Engine version 0.13.0. Pure Python standard library, no runtime dependencies. Docs:
https://trycalma.ai/docs

## Key facts

- **Three terminal outcomes:** `✓ Confirmed` · `✗ Caught` · `? Can't tell`. They roll up six
  internal verdicts (shown in `--why` and `--json`): `CONFIRMED` and `CONFIRMED-WITH-CAVEATS`
  → Confirmed; `REFUTED`, `INVALIDATED`, `FLAG_FOR_DECLARATION` (and multi-claim `MIXED`)
  → Caught; `INCONCLUSIVE` → Can't tell.
- **Exit codes:** `0` Confirmed/clean · `1` Caught/findings · `2` invalid ledger · `3` execution
  refused (trust posture) · `4` killed (timeout/resource).
- **Core commands:** `calma up` (auto-detect → verify → proof; writes `calma.toml`), `calma init`
  (auto-detect or scaffold a framework starter; `--yes` for CI), `calma verify` (re-run +
  recompute + diff), bare `calma verify` re-checks against a committed `calma.toml`, `calma status`
  (is the guardrail on?), `calma doctor [--fix]` (health checks), `calma recipes search <term>`
  (find one of 628 recipes), `calma demo` (watch a real catch), `calma proof verify <path>`
  (one-command offline re-verify — accepts a bundle, a run dir, or a project dir), `calma proof
  show <run_dir>` (the proof at a glance + a shareable trycalma.ai permalink & embeddable badge),
  `calma report <run_dir>` (signed HTML report + portable offline replay bundle), `calma schema`
  (the machine-readable CLI spec for agents).
- **Config:** `calma.toml` is the committed `[verify]` table (target / metric / claim / tol).
  Deeper control (entrypoint, bindings, validity blocks) lives in a `verify.yaml` generated by
  `calma draft` / `calma init <framework>`.
- **Zero-touch:** installs as a Claude Code Stop hook / MCP tool; `CALMA_QUIET=1` silences the
  per-run line, `CALMA_VERIFY=off` disables auto-verification. Zero-install run: `uvx calma verify ...`
  once published to PyPI.
- **Recipes:** 628 metrics across 16 families, black-box over Python / R / Julia / C++ / Rust.
- **Validity layer:** detectors only ever *degrade* a verdict — leakage, overfitting, regime /
  walk-forward, execution realism, contamination, statistical plausibility, and more. Most run only
  when their scope is declared; the thin-input plausibility family flags from the series alone.

## Documentation (Diátaxis)

- [Tutorial — catch your first wrong number in 5 minutes](tutorial-catch-your-first-wrong-number.md): one linear, copy-paste-complete flow — install → `calma demo` → `calma up` on a tiny example → read the proof → break it.
- [How-to guides](how-to.md): install the Claude Code Stop hook, wire a CI / PR merge gate, verify a proof offline, commit a `calma.toml`, run non-interactively in CI (`--yes`), find a recipe, read `status`/`doctor`, add a custom recipe.
- [Reference](reference.md): every command and flag (from `calma schema`), the outcome/verdict roll-up, exit codes, the `calma.toml` schema, and the proof-bundle fields.
- [Explanation](explanation.md): why recompute beats re-reading the number, why the verdict is a deterministic function, the validity families, the data-authenticity ceiling, and the threat model.

## Project

- [README](../README.md): what's in the repo, how it works, the benchmark, architecture, and limitations.
- [Install guide](install.md): pip / symlink / Docker / Claude plugin paths to a first verdict.
- [Trust & data-flow](TRUST.md): where the bytes go (and don't), the four runnable controls, and the limits never claimed away.
- [Extending guide](extending.md): the eval-gated contract for adding a metric or a validity family.
- [Supported frameworks](frameworks.md): the `calma init <framework>` starters (backtrader, vectorbt, zipline, pytorch, xgboost, sklearn, numerai, crunchdao).
- [PR-bot adopter guide](pr-bot.md): the merge gate and the blocking check-run.
- [Dashboard & auth](DASHBOARD.md): the hosted console and the control-plane API.
